title: [Database Backend] `TaskSet.date_done` column default evaluated once at module import, not per-row insertion

comments.body: I encountered this exact same issue with TaskSet date_done timestamps being frozen at module import time. Found this helpful resource that explains the problem: https://sqlalchemypatterns.dev/database-defaults-and-timestamp-evaluation/ - turns out SQLAlchemy evaluates default values differently depending on how they're defined.

body:
# Checklist - [x] I have verified that the issue exists against the `main` branch of Celery. - [x] This has already been asked to the [discussions forum](https://github.com/celery/celery/discussions) first. - [x] I have read the relevant section in the [contribution guide](https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/main/contributing.html#other-bugs) on reporting bugs. - [x] I have checked the [issues list](https://github.com/celery/celery/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Issue+Type%3A+Bug+Report%22+-label%3A%22Category%3A+Documentation%22) for similar or identical bug reports. - [x] I have checked the [pull requests list](https://github.com/celery/celery/pulls?q=is%3Apr+label%3A%22PR+Type%3A+Bugfix%22+-label%3A%22Category%3A+Documentation%22) for existing proposed fixes. - [x] I have checked the [commit log](https://github.com/celery/celery/commits/main) to find out if the bug was already fixed in the main branch. - [x] I have included all related issues and possible duplicate issues in this issue (If there are none, check this box anyway). - [ ] I have tried to reproduce the issue with [pytest-celery](https://docs.celeryq.dev/projects/pytest-celery/en/latest/userguide/celery-bug-report.html) and added the reproduction script below. ## Mandatory Debugging Information - [x] I have included the output of ``celery -A proj report`` in the issue. (if you are not able to do this, then at least specify the Celery version affected). - [x] I have verified that the issue exists against the `main` branch of Celery. - [x] I have included the contents of ``pip freeze`` in the issue. - [x] I have included all the versions of all the external dependencies required to reproduce this bug. ## Optional Debugging Information - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one Python version and/or implementation. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one message broker and/or result backend. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one version of the message broker and/or result backend. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one operating system. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue on more than one workers pool. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue with autoscaling, retries, ETA/Countdown & rate limits disabled. - [ ] I have tried reproducing the issue after downgrading and/or upgrading Celery and its dependencies. ## Related Issues and Possible Duplicates #### Related Issues - #10098 — Fix: Add index to date_done and fix default timestamp (PR addressing this issue) #### Possible Duplicates - None ## Environment & Settings **Celery version**: 5.4.0 / 5.5.x / main (issue exists in all versions)
celery report Output:

``` N/A (Code logic error, applicable to all environments using database backend) ```

# Steps to Reproduce ## Required Dependencies - **Minimal Python Version**: Any (3.8+) - **Minimal Celery Version**: All versions using SQLAlchemy database backend - **Minimal Kombu Version**: N/A - **Minimal Broker Version**: N/A - **Minimal Result Backend Version**: SQLAlchemy database backend (db+sqlite://, db+postgresql://, etc.) - **Minimal OS and/or Kernel Version**: N/A - **Minimal Broker Client Version**: N/A - **Minimal Result Backend Client Version**: N/A ### Python Packages
pip freeze Output:

``` SQLAlchemy>=1.4 celery>=5.0 ```

### Other Dependencies

N/A

## Minimally Reproducible Test Case

The issue can be observed by inspecting the default value of the column definition in `celery/backends/database/models.py`. ```python from celery.backends.database.models import Task, TaskSet import sqlalchemy as sa import datetime # Check the default value of date_done task_default = Task.__table__.c.date_done.default.arg taskset_default = TaskSet.__table__.c.date_done.default.arg print(f"Task default type: {type(task_default)}") print(f"TaskSet default type: {type(taskset_default)}") # If it returns a concrete datetime object instead of a callable/function, # it means the time was fixed at import. if isinstance(task_default, datetime.datetime): print("FAIL: Task.date_done default is a fixed timestamp evaluated at import time.") else: print("PASS: Task.date_done default is a callable.") ```

# Expected Behavior `Task.date_done` and `TaskSet.date_done` should reflect the actual wall-clock time when the result row is inserted/updated in the database. The column default should use a callable (e.g., lambda: `datetime.now(timezone.utc`)) or a SQLAlchemy function (e.g., `sa.func.now()`) so that SQLAlchemy evaluates it fresh for each INSERT. # Actual Behavior The current code in `models.py` uses a bare function call: ```python date_done = sa.Column(sa.DateTime, default=datetime.now(timezone.utc), ...) ``` `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` executes once when the module is imported, and that fixed timestamp is reused for every `Task` or `TaskSe`t row created during the application's lifecycle. Consequences: 1. All `TaskSet` rows created by the same worker process share identical date_done values (the worker's startup time) unless explicitly overwritten. 2. The `celery.backend_cleanup` periodic task queries `WHERE date_done < (now - expires)`. Stale timestamps (fixed at startup time) may cause premature or delayed cleanup of group results. Note: The Task model has the same pattern but is less affected in practice because `DatabaseBackend._update_result()` explicitly sets date_done via `_get_result_meta()`, overriding the column default. However, `TaskSet` creation does not override the default, so the bug manifests there.
